settings.heading: Practical business support for decisions, systems and execution
settings.body_text: Choose the right pathway across advisory, consulting, implementation, operations, resources and membership support.

settings.heading: Templates, toolkits and documentation suites for practical business use
settings.body_text: Access structured resources, supported options and practical documentation pathways for common business needs.

settings.heading: Ongoing support for business owners who need structure and momentum
settings.body_text: Compare membership options and choose the level of support that fits your operating rhythm.

settings.heading: Start with the kind of support you need
settings.body: Use the homepage as a practical router. Each pathway explains what it is for, what you get and where to go next.

settings.title: Advisory
settings.description: Decision support, business review and practical guidance.

settings.title: Consulting
settings.description: Structured support for improvement, change and operations.

settings.title: Implementation
settings.description: Help turning decisions into systems, workflows and delivery.

settings.heading: Clear pathways before you proceed
settings.body: Delivery, support and scope expectations stay clear before you choose a pathway or checkout.

settings.heading: Clear scope
settings.body: Clear inclusions and boundaries before checkout.

settings.heading: Support available
settings.body: Supported pathways available for selected resources.

settings.heading: Start from the problem in front of you
settings.text: If the right service is not obvious, match the current blocker to the pathway that can move it forward.

settings.title: A decision needs a second view
settings.text: Use Advisory before committing money, suppliers, systems, people or implementation time.

settings.title: The problem needs a plan
settings.text: Use Consulting when the issue needs diagnosis, recommendations and a roadmap before work starts.

settings.title: The plan needs delivery
settings.text: Use Implementation when the business needs setup, documentation, workflow creation or handover.

settings.title: Recurring work needs a cleaner route
settings.text: Use Operations Hub for services, controls, admin systems, operating rhythm and repeatable follow-through.

settings.title: A template or toolkit may be enough
settings.text: Use Essentials when you need a practical document, checklist or supported resource instead of a full engagement.

settings.title: The right route is unclear
settings.text: Use Support when the request involves an order, onboarding, membership, Business NBN or general routing help.

settings.heading: A practical path from need to next step
settings.text: RBP keeps the pathway clear so the business can choose, share context, receive the output or guidance, and move forward.

settings.title: Choose the pathway
settings.text: Start with advisory, consulting, implementation, operations, essentials, membership or support.

settings.title: Share the context
settings.text: Provide the order, product, business issue, timeline and outcome needed when intake or support is required.

settings.title: Receive the output or route
settings.text: The next step may be a digital resource, decision brief, plan, implementation output, support response or follow-up pathway.

settings.title: Move the work forward
settings.text: Use the result to proceed, pause, compare options, request deeper support or access a practical resource.

settings.heading: Clarify, plan, build, run, resource or resolve
settings.text: Use the stage of the work to decide where to start.

settings.title: Decision support
settings.text: Use Advisory when you need risks, trade-offs and a recommended next step.

settings.title: Roadmap support
settings.text: Use Consulting when the issue needs structure before delivery begins.

settings.title: Implementation support
settings.text: Use Implementation when the work needs setup, documents, workflows or handover.

settings.title: Operations support
settings.text: Use Operations Hub for recurring business work and practical follow-through.

settings.title: Templates and toolkits
settings.text: Ask support to help find the right template, toolkit or practical resource while the Essentials page is being assigned.

settings.title: Support routing
settings.text: Use Support for existing customer help, onboarding, membership, Business NBN or unclear next steps.

settings.heading: Support that fits real business constraints
settings.text: Use RBP when the work needs commercial context, practical sequencing, manual review, onboarding or prepared delivery. Membership can support ongoing access when the need is broader than one purchase.

settings.heading: Useful starting points before the next step
settings.text: Start with confirmed RBP destinations for resources, membership and business connectivity support.

settings.title: Essentials
settings.text: Ask support to help find templates, toolkits, checklists and supported resource options.

settings.title: Membership
settings.text: Overview of practical business pathways, resources and support access.

settings.title: Business NBN
settings.text: Business connectivity availability and next-step support.

settings.heading: Common questions before you choose
settings.body: Use these questions to understand service boundaries, resource support and how to choose the right RBP pathway.
settings.support_body: Start with support if the right pathway is unclear or the request involves an order, onboarding or existing resource.

settings.question: Which RBP pathway should I start with?
settings.answer: Start with the outcome you need. Advisory helps with decisions, Consulting helps structure a problem, Implementation helps turn a plan into working assets, Operations Hub supports recurring work, Essentials covers practical resources, and Support helps route unclear requests.

settings.question: Are templates and toolkits self-serve or supported?
settings.answer: Some resources are designed for self-serve use, and selected resources may have supported pathways where stated. Check the product or pathway page before checkout so inclusions and boundaries are clear.

settings.question: Is membership required to use RBP?
settings.answer: No. Membership is for business owners who need ongoing structure and momentum. You can still use individual pathways, resources or support without starting with membership.

settings.question: What should I send with a support request?
settings.answer: Include the business context, any relevant order or product details, timing pressure and the outcome you are trying to reach. That makes routing and next steps clearer.

settings.question: How are partner or regulated-service pathways handled?
settings.answer: Pages for partner offers or regulated-service topics should explain the role, referral pathway and support boundary before you proceed.

settings.heading: Not sure where to start?
settings.text: Send the business context, order or product details, timing pressure and outcome needed so RBP can route the request correctly.
